title: Minnesota Sues Federal Government to Access ICE Shooting Evidence
excerpt: Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison and Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty filed a federal lawsuit alleging the DOJ and DHS blocked state investigators from accessing evidence in the fatal shootings of Renee Good, Alex Pretti, and the wounding of Julio Sosa-Celis. The state argued the federal government reneged on initial pledges of investigative cooperation.
content:
On March 24, 2026, Minnesota state officials escalated their conflict with the Trump administration by filing suit in U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia against the Department of Justice, the Department of Homeland Security, Attorney General Pam Bondi, and then-DHS Secretary Kristi Noem.

  • The lawsuit alleged that federal authorities refused to share evidence in three shootings during Operation Metro Surge, including the deaths of two U.S. citizens.
  • State officials said federal agents initially agreed to collaborate at shooting scenes but later took unilateral control of all evidence.
  • In the Pretti case, federal authorities physically blocked Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension (BCA) investigators from accessing the scene entirely.
  • Evidence withheld included Pretti's cell phone, Good's vehicle — described as shrink-wrapped in an FBI warehouse — and information about the ICE officer Jonathan Ross's training history.

Minnesota Attorney General Ellison stated: "It is extraordinary that we need to file this lawsuit at all." The lawsuit was filed with pro bono assistance from the Washington Litigation Group and Georgetown Law's Institute for Constitutional Advocacy and Protection.
mediaPlaceholder: Press conference with Minnesota AG Ellison and Hennepin County Attorney Moriarty announcing the lawsuit
